---
name: Rémi
surname: Thériault
# profilepic: "img/profile.png"
position: "Chercheur postdoctoral"
qualifications: "PhD"
address: "New York University, New York, USA"
phone: (332) 400-6781
www: remi-theriault.com/fr
email: "remi.theriault@nyu.edu"
linkedin: rempsyc
orcid: "0000-0003-4315-6788"
# googlescholar: NrfwEncAAAAJ
# github: rempsyc
# twitter: RemiTPsyc
# pronouns: "il/lui"

aboutme: |
  \RaggedRight Mes intérêts se situent à l’intersection de la psychologie sociale, des neurosciences et de la psychologie positive, et mes recherches se penchent sur le bien-être optimal, la cohésion sociale, et notre rapport à l’environnement. Dans mes recherches postdoctorales, je tente de réduire la polarisation politique via le développement d'identités sociales partagées. Je suis également passionné par la science ouverte, la science reproductible, la métascience et la science des données à l’aide de R (ce CV a aussi été [réalisé en R](https://github.com/rempsyc/cv)).

headcolor: "6FA3CE" # my shirt on the profil pic is 839AC4, but we take a bit lighter for better look (6FA3CE)
# Ok recent feedback was to not use colour at all here, so instead we go with the dark blue of my suit
# (21242D) so it doesn't even look that obvious but we still have a little something.
# #333333 is the default grey.
urlcolor: blue
linkcolor: blue
output: 
  vitae::awesomecv:
    page_total: true
    font_scale: 1.0
header-includes:
  - \usepackage[gen]{eurosym}
  - \usepackage[main=french]{babel}
   # We'll use totpages (not lastpage) to get total pages, avoids duplicate-label warnings
  - \usepackage{totpages}
  # Hook AwesomeCV's footer and rewrite its 3rd argument (page text)
  - \makeatletter
  - \let\origmakecvfooter\makecvfooter
  - \renewcommand{\makecvfooter}[3]{\origmakecvfooter{#1}{#2}{\thepage/\ref{TotPages}}}
  - \makeatother
lang: french
date: "\\today"
papersize: letterpaper
---

```{r setup, include = FALSE}
# Make sure to install babel TeX package
# tinytex::tlmgr_install("babel-french")

# Windows French locale for date
try(Sys.setlocale("LC_TIME", "French_France.1252"), silent = TRUE)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = FALSE, warning = FALSE, message = FALSE)

###################################
#### YOUR SETTINGS (MUST EDIT) ####
###################################

# Language: "EN" for English, "FR" for French
language <- "FR"

# Whether to print a shortened version of the CV
short <- FALSE

# How your name appears in publications (used for auto-bolding)
author_bold <- "Thériault, R."

# Your Google Scholar profile ID (from your profile URL)
scholar.profile <- "NrfwEncAAAAJ"

# Your Google Scholar short author name
author.name <- "rém thériault"
# Note: correct name may vary (e.g., if you inconsistently used middle name initials)
# Dominique Makowski's was simply "d makowski", so the pattern is unclear
# But usually it seems 1-3 letters from first name plus full last name
# When in doubt, the correct author name can be obtained through:
# get_scholar_name(scholar.profile) # (after defining 'scholar.profile' above)

###############################
#### OPTIONAL SETTINGS ########
###############################

# Cache settings: avoid hitting Google Scholar on every knit
refresh_scholar <- FALSE  # Set to TRUE to force a fresh fetch
cache_days <- 1           # Re-fetch automatically after this many days

# Publications to exclude from Google Scholar (e.g., preprints already listed elsewhere)
# Use format: "LastName (Year)" — add letter suffix for disambiguation: "LastName (Yearb)"
scholar_exclude <- c("Makowski (2023)", "Lüdecke (2023)")

# Fix author name inconsistencies from Google Scholar (named vector: wrong = correct)
scholar_name_fixes <- c("R Thériault" = "Rém Thériault")

# Fix author ORDER for specific publications (named list: wrong order = correct order)
# Useful for co-first-author papers where you want to be listed first so it counts
# toward your number of first-author publications in the impact metrics.
# Use pubids from your Google Scholar profile URL (click a paper → see ?pubid= in URL)
scholar_author_corrections <- list(
  # Swap co-first authors so this paper counts as first-author:
  "M Miglianico, Rém Thériault" = "Rém Thériault, M Miglianico"
)

# Append missing author to specific publications (vector of pubids)
scholar_append_author <- c("Zph67rFs4hoC", "IWHjjKOFINEC")

# Header color for the CV
headcolor <- "0a0a0a"

# Keep numbering for publications by default. Toggle to TRUE when you want
# numbering in talks, posters, awards, grants, software, etc. for easy counting.
count_everything <- FALSE

# Local size knobs that avoid changing the overall document font scale.
references_text_size <- "\\small"

########################################
#### PACKAGE SETUP (DO NOT MODIFY) #####
########################################

# Loads and auto-installs if needed:
#   vitae, tidyverse, scholar, patchwork, see, rsvg, kableExtra, here
source("files/packages.R")

# To format links throughout the CV
source("files/functions.R")

########################
##### INSTRUCTIONS #####
########################

# Change the order of sections below as desired.
# Edit the individual files ("childs") to edit the content as desired.
# Add `eval = !short` to code chunks to make them optional for the short version

# See all instructions and tips here: https://github.com/rempsyc/cv

```

```{r Appointments parent, child=c('sections/appointments.Rmd')}
```

```{r Education parent, child=c('sections/education.Rmd')}
```

```{r Grants parent, child=c('sections/grants.Rmd')}
```

\pagebreak

```{r Awards parent, child=c('sections/awards.Rmd')}
```

```{r Publications parent, child=c('sections/publications.Rmd')}
```

```{r Preprints parent, child=c('sections/preprints.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Teaching parent, child=c('sections/teaching.Rmd')}
```

```{r Invited talks parent, child=c('sections/invited_talks.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Conference talks parent, child=c('sections/conference_talks.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Conference posters parent, child=c('sections/conference_posters.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Academic service parent, child=c('sections/service.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Software parent, child=c('sections/software.Rmd')}
```

```{r Media coverage parent, child=c('sections/media_coverage.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r Blogs parent, child=c('sections/blogs.Rmd'), eval = TRUE}
```

```{r Other experience parent, child=c('sections/other_experience.Rmd'), eval = TRUE}
```

```{r References parent, child=c('sections/references.Rmd'), eval = !short}
```

```{r credit parent, child=c('sections/credit.Rmd'), eval = TRUE}
```

<!-- We skip the sections below for now. They are on the website and it was recommended to avoid them for the CV for applying to prof positions -->
